data.title: Tech Voices: OpenAI warns on stock tokens, CoreWeave-Nvidia, Apple
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's daily roundup of statements and remarks that could impact the technology sector.

  • OpenAI and Elon Musk are advising investors that Robinhood's (NASDAQ:HOOD) OpenAI tokens are not actual equity stakes in the privately held company. 

"These 'OpenAI tokens' are not OpenAI equity. We did not partner with Robinhood, were not involved in this, and do not endorse it.  Any transfer of OpenAI equity requires our approval—we did not approve any transfer. Please be careful," OpenAI said in a post on X. 

"Your 'equity' is fake," Musk added, in his own post on X. Musk, who currently serves as CEO of Tesla (TLSA), SpaceX (SPACE) and xAI, is a co-founder of OpenAI. 

Robinhood (NASDAQ:HOOD) announced earlier this week that it would be offering tokens for over 200 U.S. stocks and ETFs.

  • Nvidia-backed (NASDAQ:NVDA) CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) said it is the first AI cloud provider to deploy Nvidia GB300 NVL72 AI server systems.

"CoreWeave is constantly working to push the boundaries of AI development further, deploying the bleeding-edge cloud capabilities required to train the next generation of AI models," said CoreWeave Co-Founder and CTO Peter Salanki, in a statement. "We're proud to be the first to stand up this transformative platform and help innovators prepare for the next exciting wave of AI."

CoreWeave said it collaborated with Dell (NYSE:DELL), Switch, and Vertiv (NYSE:VRT) to build the initial deployment of the NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 systems. CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) held its initial public offering in March. 

  • Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) iPhone sales in China increased for the first time in two years last quarter, according to CNBC, which cited Counterpoint Research.

“Apple’s adjustment of iPhone prices in May was well-timed and well received, coming a week ahead of the 618 shopping festival," said Counterpoint Research Associate Director Ethan Qi, in a statement.

data.title: REE Automotive receives Nasdaq minimum bid price notification
data.description:
• REE Automotive (NASDAQ:REE) on Thursday said it has received a letter from Nasdaq, indicating that the company is currently not in compliance with Nasdaq rules.
  • In particular, the letter stated that the company’s closing bid price for its Class A ordinary shares has been below $1.00 per share for the last 30 consecutive business days beginning on May 15, 2025 through June 27, 2025.
  • According to the letter, the company has until December 29, 2025 to regain compliance with the minimum bid price requirement.
  • REE +1.47% after hours to $0.69.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Appian reports insider transactions worth ~$6.25M
data.description:
Software platform provider Appian (NASDAQ:APPN) reported insider transactions worth ~$6.25M.

The company said its 10% owner Abdiel Capital Advisors sold 206,722 class A shares of indirect ownership in multiple transactions at prices ranging from $29.59 to $30.79.

data.title: Rio Tinto-backed aluminum company plans C$1.5B Canada smelter project - Bloomberg
data.description:
Rio Tinto-backed (NYSE:RIO) Aluminerie Alouette is planning to commit up to C$1.5B (~US$1.1B) to modernize its facilities in northern Quebec, and has secured a new electricity supply deal with the province's Hydro-Quebec utility, Bloomberg reported Wednesday.

Aluminerie Alouette is a consortium made up of Rio Tinto (NYSE:RIO), which owns 40%, and other companies including Norway’s Hydro Aluminum; its smelter in Sept-Îles, Quebec, has capacity of 630K metric tons/year of primary aluminum.

The project will be welcome news for an industry facing pressures from President Trump's trade war, which has imposed 50% tariffs on foreign-produced aluminum in a move that will affect U.S. businesses that use the metal.

For example, beer and alcohol maker Constellation Brands said Wednesday that it expects aluminum tariffs to cost the company ~$20M during the remainder of its fiscal year.

Canada produce 70% of all aluminum in North America and is the largest exporter of the metal to the U.S., which lacks aluminum smelting capacity meet its own needs.

data.title: Welltower reports insider transaction worth ~$3M
data.description:
Welltower (NYSE:WELL) said in an SEC filing that director Andrew Gundlach acquired ~$3M worth shares of indirect ownership in the health care REIT.

Gundlach purchased 20K shares of common stock in multiple trades at prices ranging from $151.23 to $151.77.

data.title: S&P 500 ends about 20 points shy of 6,300 level ahead of Independence Day holiday weekend
data.description:
Wall Street notched another record close on Thursday, as market participants snapped up equities and dumped bonds after nonfarm payrolls jumped more than expected in June.

Trading volume was low, as the U.S. stock market closed early on account of the July 4 Independence Day holiday.

Both the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) closed at all-time peaks, ending +0.8% and +1%, respectively. The indices settled at 6,279.35 points and 20,601.10 points, respectively. 

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) closed +0.8%, less than 190 points away from its record close of 45,014.04 points.

June nonfarm payrolls came in at +147K vs. +110K consensus and +144K in May (revised from +139K), while the unemployment rate was 4.1% vs. 4.3% consensus and +4.2% prior.

The stronger-than-expected jobs report calmed fears of a slowdown in the U.S. economy. It also quelled expectations of an interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve in July.

"If today’s short session taught us anything, it’s that markets don’t have to worry about growth falling off a cliff. Although today’s jobs showed more than 70,000 new government jobs (147,000 total NFP gains), it was a sign of strength that cyclical areas like manufacturing did not show tariff-related fatigue,” Leo Nelissen, part of investing group iREIT on Alpha, told Seeking Alpha.

“As a result, bond yields rose, and the dollar strengthened a bit, two signs of economic resilience. It’s also fair to say the Fed was right to wait before cutting,” Nelissen said.

“Although none of today’s numbers were in any way groundbreaking for the market, we continue to enjoy an environment where we can assume that double-digit EPS growth for the S&P 500 will hold," he added.

Over in the bond market, yields jumped as bonds sold off. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 7 basis points to 4.35%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 9 basis points to 3.89%.

Also on Thursday's economic calendar, initial jobless claims unexpectedly slipped in the previous week, while the U.S. goods and services trade deficit expanded in May. 

U.S. stocks closed at a record high on Wednesday, after U.S. President Donald Trump said a trade deal had been reached with Vietnam.

data.title: Vertex unveils longer-term data on Casgevy
data.description:
• Vertex Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:VRTX) said that new data on Casgevy (exagamglogene autotemcel) demonstrates the benefits of the gene therapy for severe sickle cell disease or transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia over the long term. 
  • For sickle cell disease, 95.6% of evaluable patients (those with at least 16 months of follow-up) had no vaso-occlusive crises for at least 12 consecutive months in two trials. The mean duration of VOC-free was 35 months.
  • For transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia, 98.2% of evaluable patients saw a transfusion-independence for at least 12 consecutive months with a weighted average hemoglobin of at least 9 g/dL in two trials. The mean duration of transfusion independence was 40.5 months.
  • The new data was presented at the European Hematology Association Congress.

data.title: CoreWeave becomes first hyperscaler to deploy Nvidia's GB300 NVL72
data.description:
CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) has become the first hyperscaler to deploy Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) GB300 NVL72 liquid-cooled, rack-scale AI system, the company announced today.

CoreWeave shares surged 6% by noon trading on Thursday. Nvidia had edged up 1.4%. Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL), another partner involved in the system, was up 1.7%.

The GB300 NVL72 features 72 Nvidia Blackwell Ultra GPUs and 36 Arm-based (ARM) Nvidia Grace CPUs. The unit is housed within Dell's integrated rack scale system. The system is 10 times faster in user responsiveness and has a five-time improvement in throughput per watt over the prior generation of Nvidia's Hopper.

It is built for frontier-scale AI and allows users to train, optimize and deploy multi-trillion parameter models, CoreWeave said. It's also engineered to handle test-time scaling inference, which is crucial for deploying advanced AI models.

Last month, Supermicro (SMCI) unveiled a range of enterprise AI solutions built on Nvidia's Blackwell architecture to accelerate AI factory deployments in the European market. The company introduced more than 30 solutions designed for air or liquid-cooled NVIDIA HGX B200, liquid-cooled NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 and NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition.

Core Scientific (CORZ), a long-time partner of CoreWeave, inched down about 1%. Last month, reports surfaced that CoreWeave was considering acquiring the company, prompting a stock surge. 

CoreWeave launched its initial public offering in late March 2025. Its stock value has quadrupled since its IPO date.

data.title: BofA ups PT for Roku on Amazon partnership, improving advertiser sentiment
data.description:
Analysts at Bank of America hiked their price target on Roku (NASDAQ:ROKU) and reiterated their bullish rating, citing improving macroeconomic sentiment and viewing its recently announced Amazon (AMZN) partnership as favorable.

The research firm said the uncertainty seen in the last few months for advertising trends "appears to be abating," and "sentiment has improved" as the second quarter progressed. 

They see the Amazon partnership "as another proof point that Roku intends to be more flexible and drive further interoperability within their platform."

The partnership will provide a significant opportunity as monetization of their advertising inventory improves going forward and further deepens their relationships with other DSPs, BofA said.

"Roku has notable scope to keep expanding its top and bottom line and should benefit from several favorable industry trends along with company-specific actions," BofA said in its July 3 research note. "These include 1) growth in CTV advertising, 2) secular growth in streaming video domestically and internationally, and 3) improving fill rates (the % of ad spaces being successfully filled) in its advertising business."

BofA raised its 2025 revenue forecast for Roku to $4.59B, from $4.55B. It also upped its FY EBITDA forecast to $360M from a prior guide of $351M.

ROKU has a PT of $110, up from $100, implying an upside of 27.3%. Stock is up 2.2% by midday on Thursday.
